# Cosmological singularity from entangled CFTs

## Core Research Question
Can cosmological singularities be derived or sharply diagnosed from entangled states of holographic CFT sectors, and can boundary entanglement observables distinguish singularity resolution from singularity avoidance?

## Current Focus
Benchmark the verified entangled-CFT cosmology papers against Kasner/QES/two-point-function diagnostics and isolate a boundary observable that could distinguish singularity resolution from singularity avoidance.

## Literature Anchors
- Stefano Antonini, Martin Sasieta, Brian Swingle, "Cosmology from random entanglement," arXiv:2307.14416, JHEP 11 (2023) 188.
- A. Manu, K. Narayan, Partha Paul, "Cosmological singularities, entanglement and quantum extremal surfaces," arXiv:2012.07351, JHEP 04 (2021) 200.
- Yonatan Frenkel, Sean A. Hartnoll, Jorrit Kruthoff, Zixiang Shi, "Holographic flows from CFT to the Kasner universe," arXiv:2004.01192, JHEP 08 (2020) 003.
- Netta Engelhardt, Thomas Hertog, Gary T. Horowitz, "Holographic Signatures of Cosmological Singularities," arXiv:1404.2309, Phys. Rev. Lett. 113 (2014) 121602.
- K. Narayan, Hitesh K. Saini, Gopal Yadav, "Cosmological singularities, holographic complexity and entanglement," arXiv:2404.00761, JHEP 07 (2024) 125.
- Abhisek Sahu, Mark Van Raamsdonk, "Holographic black hole cosmologies," arXiv:2411.14673, JHEP 05 (2025) 233.

## Target Results
- Produce a literature matrix linking entangled-CFT cosmology to established holographic singularity probes.
- Fix a shortlist of boundary observables and scope limits for the project.
- Build a phased roadmap for derivations, benchmarks, and verification.
